summary: Chromium cookie and saved-password extractor distributed as an npm CLI
details: Package ships a Windows-targeted offensive tool that decrypts and dumps Chromium-based browser (Chrome/Edge/Brave) cookies and saved passwords, including App-Bound v20 keys via a debugger-attach path implemented in a native addon. Exports `extractCookiesNetscape`, `extractPasswordsToFiles`, and addon methods `extractKey` / `decryptCookie`; output is written locally to `./<browser>_cookies/` and `./<browser>_passwords/`. The tool only runs when the installer explicitly invokes the `baksen` CLI (no install-time lifecycle beyond `node-gyp rebuild` of the shipped native source), and the only outbound network request is an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded raw.githubusercontent.com URL under a personal account (`tabo1337/SaatPCBKod/.../key.txt`) used as a mutable license-key allowlist, cached in `~/.baksen_cache` for 24h. No installer data, env vars, or extracted credentials are transmitted off-host in the code path observed. This is a dual-use credential-extraction utility rather than a covert supply-chain attack against the installer, but the capability (local browser credential theft) and the author-controlled remote allowlist warrant human distribution-policy review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
